Greenfield Farmers Market Vendors

Greenfield Farmers Market is home to more than forty regular vendors every Saturday. Our vendor community spans several generations of family farming, artisan food production, and sustainable growing practices. All vendors are required to produce or grow everything they sell within a one-hundred-mile radius of Greenfield Town Square.

Produce Farms

Sunrise Valley Farm

Sunrise Valley Farm has been a founding member of Greenfield Farmers Market for over eighteen years. The Henriksen family grows certified organic vegetables including heirloom tomatoes, sweet corn, summer squash, kale, chard, collard greens, and a wide variety of salad greens. Their stall is located at pitch A1, the first stall on the left as you enter the market.

Cedar Ridge Organics

Cedar Ridge Organics specialises in root vegetables and winter squash. You will find parsnips, carrots, beets, celeriac, turnips, butternut squash, and delicata squash at their stand from September through November. During summer months they sell their popular spring onion bunches and garlic braids, all grown without synthetic pesticides or fertilisers.

Maple Hill Farm

Maple Hill Farm grows strawberries, raspberries, and blueberries throughout the summer season. From June through August their stand is one of the most popular at the market. They also offer fresh-pressed apple juice and frozen fruit packs in autumn. The farm has been certified organic since 2009.

Meat and Dairy

Rolling Meadows Ranch

Rolling Meadows Ranch offers pasture-raised beef, pork, and lamb. All animals are raised without antibiotics or added hormones on open pasture across their four-hundred-acre property. They offer whole and half shares for serious buyers and sell individual cuts at the market each week. Their beef is dry-aged a minimum of twenty-one days for superior flavour and tenderness.

Blue Sky Dairy

Blue Sky Dairy sells raw milk, aged cheddar, fresh chèvre, seasonal yoghurt, and cultured butter. Their mixed herd of Nubian goats and Jersey cows graze on certified organic pasture year-round. Raw milk sales comply with all state regulations and are available to adult purchasers only. Blue Sky is one of only twelve raw milk dairies licensed to sell directly to consumers in this state.

Bakery and Preserves

Heartland Bakehouse

Heartland Bakehouse bakes sourdough loaves, rye bread, cinnamon rolls, and seasonal fruit tarts using locally milled heritage grain flours sourced from Stonemill Grains in the next county. All baking is done in a wood-fired stone oven on the farm. Bread is available in limited quantities and sells out quickly — arrive early for the best selection.

Grandma Rosa's Kitchen

Grandma Rosa's Kitchen offers small-batch jams, pickles, relishes, hot sauces, and fermented vegetables made from produce sourced directly from fellow market vendors. Rosa Delgado has been making preserves for over thirty years, and her bread-and-butter pickles won the state fair ribbon four years running. All products are made in a licensed commercial kitchen.

Flowers and Plants

Wildbloom Flower Farm

Wildbloom Flower Farm brings fresh-cut seasonal flowers and potted native plants to the market from May through October. Their offerings change each week depending on what is in bloom, and include sunflowers, zinnias, dahlias, snapdragons, and ornamental grasses. They also offer native pollinator plant starts in spring for home gardens.
